issue_title: Writing non blocking command line client
issue_body:
I am trying to create command line client for websocket message. User can type the message and mean while if any message is received it should be printed to terminal.

Here is a example using `gevent`.

```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
# cli.py

import sys
import os
import fcntl

import json
import urllib
import urlparse

import gevent
from gevent.socket import wait_read
from gevent.event import Event

import select

from ws4py.client.geventclient import WebSocketClient
from ws4py.exc import HandshakeError


class StdioPipedWebSocketHelper:
    def received_message(self, websocket, m):
        if m.is_binary:
            print("Binary data receieved. Skipping")
        else:
            sys.stdout.write(m.data)
        sys.stdout.write("\n")
        sys.stdout.flush()

    def opened(self, websocket):
        def connect_stdin():
            sys.stdout.write("\n")
            fcntl.fcntl(sys.stdin, fcntl.F_SETFL, os.O_NONBLOCK)
            while True:
                wait_read(sys.stdin.fileno())
                print(">")
                buf = sys.stdin.readline()
                if len(buf) == 0:
                    break
                print("[sending payload of length %d]" % (len(buf)))
                websocket.send_msg(buf)

        # XXX: We wait for the socket to open before reading stdin so that we
        # support behaviour like: echo foo | wssh -l ...
        gevent.spawn(connect_stdin)
        print("done")

    def closed(self):
        print("Connection closed by peer.")
        print("Shutting down...")
        exit(0)


class Client(WebSocketClient):
    def __init__(self, credentials):
        url = credentials.pop('url')
        qs = urllib.urlencode(credentials)
        full_url = url + "?" + qs
        print("Connecting: {}".format(full_url))
        WebSocketClient.__init__(self, full_url)

        self.shutdown_cond = Event()
        self.io = StdioPipedWebSocketHelper()

    def opened(self):
        self.io.opened(self)

    def closed(self, closed, reason):
        self.io.closed()
        self.shutdown_cond.set()

    def received_message(self, message):
        msg_obj = parse_msg(message)
        self.io.received_message(message=msg_obj)

    def send_msg(message):
        msg = json.dumps({'post': message})
        self.send(msg, False)

    def connect_and_wait(self):
        self.connect()
        self.shutdown_cond.wait()


def start(credentials):
    """Connect to the serve and keep running.

    :param credentials: `dict` containing details about server and tokens.
    """
    client = Client(credentials=credentials)
    try:
        client.connect_and_wait()
    except (IOError, HandshakeError), e:
        print >> sys.stderr, e

    

if __name__ == "__main__":
    credentials = {'url': 'ws://echo.websocket.org', 'foo': 'bar'}
    start(credentials=credentials)

```

Most of the code is borrowed from @progrium's work on `wssh`. `wssh` repo no more work because of  old version of `ws4py`.

```
(ws4py_tests)➜  ws4py_test  python cli.py
Connecting: ws://echo.websocket.org?foo=bar
done
foo
foo
bar
^CConnection closed by peer.
Shutting down...
```
After connecting to server, code is blocked inside `websocket.once`.

#### Trace

 --- modulename: greenlet, funcname: start
greenlet.py(186):         if self._start_event is None:
greenlet.py(187):             self._start_event = self.parent.loop.run_callback(self.switch)
greenlet.py(203):         return g
cli.py(46):         print("done")
done

 --- modulename: websocket, funcname: terminated

issue_title: remote callbacks not working properly
issue_body:
Today i added callbacks to my application but i came across two major issues.

1) when using the async manager the `_return_callback` function is wrapped in a partial. The asyncio.iscoroutinefunction tests whether the function is a coroutine when the callback is executed, but the partial (wrongly) is not classified as a coroutinefunction. 

2) the callbacks are returned into the message queue not with the original hosts id (which sent the message), but the host_id of the receiving server. Due to this the callback is not executed on the original host.

I have fixed the mentioned issues (https://github.com/rettier/python-socketio/commit/22a5f37f1c573e738e2e09ea369e07e39f17a290) so it works. I don't know if i have considered all cases. A lot of test cases currently fail due to the different message format, but if the fixes are desired i will wrap this up into a decent PR.

issue_title: ErrorDetail cannot be rendered to yaml
issue_body:
When you raise a ``NotFound`` exception in django rest framework, the YAML renderer triggers an internal server error. So when you do the following in a view:
```python
raise exceptions.NotFound("some error message")
```
It will raise this error:
```
...
  File "virtualenv/lib/python3.4/site-packages/rest_framework_yaml/encoders.py", line 38, in represent_mapping
    node_value = self.represent_data(item_value)
  File "virtualenv/lib/python3.4/site-packages/yaml/representer.py", line 57, in represent_data
    node = self.yaml_representers[None](self, data)
  File "virtualenv/lib/python3.4/site-packages/yaml/representer.py", line 229, in represent_undefined
    raise RepresenterError("cannot represent an object: %s" % data)
yaml.representer.RepresenterError: cannot represent an object: some error message
```
It's because the error detail of an ``APIException`` is turned into an ``ErrorDetail`` instance which is a ``str`` subclass. ``PyYaml`` doesn't recognize this and fails. This can be fixed by adding a representer.

```python 
from rest_framework import exceptions
from rest_framework_yaml.encoders import SafeDumper
from yaml.representer import SafeRepresenter

SafeDumper.add_representer(exceptions.ErrorDetail, SafeRepresenter.represent_str)
```

issue_body:

**Environment variables set:**
VAULT_ADDR=https://my.vault.install:8200/
VAULT_CACERT=/Users/atrepca/.certs/vault-ca.pem

This works fine:
```yml

With a `CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED` error:

fatal: [localhost]: FAILED! => {"msg": "An unhandled exception occurred while templating '{{ lookup('hashivault', 'secret/foo', 'bar') }}'. Error was a <class 'ansible.errors.AnsibleError'>, original message: An unhandled exception occurred while running the lookup plugin 'hashivault'. Error was a <class 'ansible.errors.AnsibleError'>, original message: Error reading vault/secret/foo/bar: Exception: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='my.vault.install', port=8200): Max retries exceeded with url: /v1/secret/foo (Caused by SSLError(SSLError(1, u'[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:590)'),))\n"}

issue_labels: [{'id': 656403030, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ni/nimi-python/labels/API', 'name': 'API', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}, {'id': 742577049, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ni/nimi-python/labels/nidcpower', 'name': 'nidcpower', 'color': '363fa0', 'default': False}, {'id': 780102262, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ni/nimi-python/labels/nidmm', 'name': 'nidmm', 'color': '6633a0', 'default': False}, {'id': 733087933, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ni/nimi-python/labels/nifgen', 'name': 'nifgen', 'color': 'ffcccf', 'default': False}, {'id': 735743004, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ni/nimi-python/labels/niscope', 'name': 'niscope', 'color': '3f5cc6', 'default': False}, {'id': 771497718, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ni/nimi-python/labels/niswitch', 'name': 'niswitch', 'color': '7fe871', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Once #718 is merged, consider our API:

### Issue 1
```python
dmm_session = nidmm.Session('Dev1')
dc_session = nidcpower.Session('Dev2')
scope_session = niscope.Session('Dev3')

# channel attributes are accessed by indexing the channels container.
l = dc_session.channels[0].current_level

# device attributes are accessed directly on the session.
c = dc_session.channel_count

# But this is equivalent. Does it matter?
c = dc_session.channels[''].channel_count
```
Does it matter?

### Issue 2
Another potentially confusing problem is that all attribute are accessed on the `Session` but also by indexing the `channels` container:

```python
# Sets range on all channels
scope_session.range = 5.0
# Set range only on channel 1
scope_session.channels[1].range = 2.0
```
Does it matter?

### Issue 3
Functions that are channel based are similar to attributes. But those that aren't (i.e. `commit()`) are only available on the `Session`, not the `channels` container., except that functions that are _not
```python
scope_session.fetch(...) # OK
scope_session.channels[0]fetch(...) # OK
scope_session.commit() # OK
scope_session.channels[0].commit() # Raises!
```
Does it matter?

### Issue 4

Some suggested to follow nidaqmx-python precedent and have an "all_channels" member.

```python
# Sets range on all channels
scope_session.all_channels.range = 5.0
```
But then what about `nidmm` which has one channel (some would say no channels) and most of the C/LV API doesn't even have channel inputs? This is hardly ideal:
```python
dmm_session.all_channels.range = 10.0 # What channels?
```

issue_title: Make demo program more robust
issue_body:
The demo program ought to adhere t0
1) It leaves the target acct untouched (aka demo cleans up after itself)
2) If a target acct doesn't support some functionality, then the demo program gracefully avoids that functionality.
3) If demo program is interrupted, and thus leaves "turds" in the target acct... a subsequent run of the demo program should not barf. It should gracefully clean up that earlier stuff.

issue_title: The sigmoid definition in the tutorial is wrong
issue_body:
Hi there, 

Just a small point.

Reading through the [tutorial](https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/docs/tutorial.md), the definition of sigmoid is given in terms of tanh as

```
def sigmoid(x):
    return 0.5*(np.tanh(x) + 1)
```

But should it not be

```
def sigmoid(x):
    return 0.5*(np.tanh(x/2) + 1)
```

For example sigmoid(1) with the first method gives ~0.88, with the second method gives the expected ~0.73.

This obviously isn't a huge issue but could be misleading if people just used the example code without double checking!

obj = claripy.BVS('symbolic_obj', 64)
state.memory.store(state.regs.rsp, obj, endness=endness)

for k in range(len(obj) / 8):

	print "Offset: " + str(k)

	v2 = obj[(8 * (k + 1)) - 1: (8 * (k))]
	b2 = sorted(state.se.any_n_int(v2, 260))

	v1 = state.memory.load(state.regs.rsp + k, 1, endness=endness)

issue_title: boto3 ECS client run_task doesn't set cpu or memory overrides.
issue_body:
When I try and override the CPU or memory limits when calling ecs_client.run_task() the client silently accepts the parameters but they are not reflected in the ECS UI when viewing the started task.

This code works but the started task will not have the specified cpu or memory limits. 
```python
import boto3

client = boto3.client('ecs')

client.run_task(
            cluster='default',

            "name": "my-task",

        )

If I try and pass the memory or cpu parameters in as strings then I get parameter validation exceptions so I am pretty sure boto3 is aware of the parameters.

issue_title: GameLift client "create_build" returns partial upload information
issue_body:
In the boto3 client for the GameLift service, the `create_build` call returns partial upload information.  If I run this code:

```
gamelift_client = boto3.client('gamelift',
					      aws_access_key_id=AWS_ACCESS_KEY,
					      aws_secret_access_key=AWS_SECRET_KEY,
					      region_name=GAMELIFT_REGION)
create_build_result = gamelift_client.create_build(Name='some-build-name',Version='1')
```

I get this result:

```
{
	'ResponseMetadata' : {
		'HTTPStatusCode' : 200,
		'RequestId' : '<redacted>'
	},
	u 'Build' : {
		u 'Status' : u 'INITIALIZED',
		u 'Name' : u '<redacted>',
		u 'BuildId' : u '<redacted>',
		u 'CreationTime' : datetime.datetime(2016, 2, 29, 11, 21, 33, 706000, tzinfo = tzlocal()),
		u 'SizeOnDisk' : 0,
		u 'Version' : u '1'
	},
	u 'UploadCredentials' : {
		u 'SecretAccessKey' : u '<redacted>',
		u 'SessionToken' : u '<redacted>',
		u 'AccessKeyId' : u '<redacted>'
	}
}
```

So GameLift returns me the UploadCredentials I need to initiate an upload, but not the actual S3 StorageLocation for me to make the upload.  This seems to conflict with the published API docs for the CreateBuild operation:  https://docs.aws.amazon.com/gamelift/latest/apireference/API_CreateBuild.html

Then I call this code after creating a build:

```
build_id = create_build_result['Build']['BuildId']
request_creds_response = gamelift_client.request_upload_credentials(BuildId=build_id)
```

And I get this result:

```
{
	u 'StorageLocation' : {
		u 'Bucket' : u 'prod-gamescale-builds-us-west-2',
		u 'Key' : u '<redacted>'
	},
	'ResponseMetadata' : {
		'HTTPStatusCode' : 200,
		'RequestId' : '<redacted>'
	},
	u 'UploadCredentials' : {
		u 'SecretAccessKey' : u '<redacted>',
		u 'SessionToken' : u '<redacted>',
		u 'AccessKeyId' : u '<redacted>'
	}
}
```

This call successfully returns both the UploadCredentials and the StorageLocation.

So, my question is, why does `create_build` only return the UploadCredentials and not the StorageLocation?  It seems to me it should either return both of those values or neither of those values.  The way it is now isn't broken per se, but it is really confusing.

As a side note, the AccessKeyId and SecretAccessKey returned by `create_build` and `request_upload_credentials` are different too, so it seems like GameLift might be unnecessarily generating credentials twice.

issue_title: bucket name with "." not DNS compatible, connect timeout
issue_body:
env: python(2.7), boto3 (1.2.3), botocore (1.3.22)

I want to upload a file to a bucket named 'mybucket.com' in Tokyo, but it always raised ConnectTimeout error. I enabled the debug mode and it shows that 'Not changing URI, bucket is not DNS compatible: auto3d.test'.

I checked the source code in botocore/utils.py and fount this comment in method check_dns_name:

    """
    Check to see if the ``bucket_name`` complies with the
    restricted DNS naming conventions necessary to allow
    access via virtual-hosting style.

    Even though "." characters are perfectly valid in this DNS
    naming scheme, we are going to punt on any name containing a
    "." character because these will cause SSL cert validation
    problems if we try to use virtual-hosting style addressing.
    """

So, at present I can't manage bucket with '.' ? Or is there any other better way to manage this bucket in python? 

BTW, I used this bucket as a static website, so I can't rename the bucket without '.' like 'mybucket'. I just want a python script to help me deploy web files easily.

Thanks.

issue_title: Documentation Error
issue_body:
Hi, 

The [documentation for Firehose](http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/firehose.html#Firehose.Client.put_record) states:

```
If the PutRecord operation throws a ServiceUnavailableException , back off and retry. If the exception persists, it is possible that the throughput limits have been exceeded for the delivery stream.
```

Neither `boto3` nor `botocore` seem to actually have a `ServiceUnavailableException` to catch though! The `PutRecords` call can return an error code `ServiceUnavailable`, but since `PutRecords` can't return error codes I am at a loss as to how I can deal with exceeded throughput limits.

Can anyone help?

Cheers,
Craig

issue_title: cc1plus error while installing Python DyNet Module
issue_body:
Hello everyone,
I'm trying to install Python DyNet module on Linux following [these steps](http://dynet.readthedocs.io/en/latest/python.html). Cmake is run without any errors and all the build files are written into dynet/build directory. But I'm getting the following error messages while running "make - j 2":
-----------
cc1plus: error: invalid option argument ‘-Ofast’
cc1plus: error: unrecognized command line option "-std=c++11"
make[2]: *** [dynet/CMakeFiles/dynet.dir/cfsm-builder.cc.o] Error 1
make[1]: *** [dynet/CMakeFiles/dynet.dir/all] Error 2
-----------
My g++ (GCC) version is 4.9.2 and I'm using Python version 2.6. 
Do you have any idea how I should address the error? Thanks!

We should make sure that it is obvious how to download training data, and that the model is at least as good as the PyTorch example:

issue_title: Speed of tagget example is slow on GPU as compared to CPU
issue_body:
This is for CoNLL-2003 datasets for NER tasks.

Speed of BiLSTM tagger example on CPU: 6.033s

Also the nvprof profiler is getting killed after 1 optimization step.

issue_title: Router ERROR Missing template
issue_body:
Our Deis cluster was provisioned with Deis 1.9.0 on 8/20 on AWS using the stateless-platform.
This morning 8/24 prior to anyone coming into work we started receiving the following on 2 of out 3 of our Deis routers.  The fix was to shutdown the router and restart as a new container.  I also jumped into the containers that were having the error to find that the files were actually present.  This error caused any traffic that was routed to these routers to fail and the router to still be considered healthy as it was responding to pings from the AWS ELB.

```
2015-08-24T13:37:41Z 9c2a97b4651e confd[46]: ERROR Missing template: /app/templates/deis.cert
2015-08-24T13:37:41Z 9c2a97b4651e confd[46]: ERROR Missing template: /app/templates/deis.conf
2015-08-24T13:37:41Z 9c2a97b4651e confd[46]: ERROR Missing template: /app/templates/deis.key
2015-08-24T13:37:41Z 9c2a97b4651e confd[46]: ERROR Missing template: /app/templates/dhparam.pem
2015-08-24T13:37:41Z 9c2a97b4651e confd[46]: ERROR Missing template: /app/templates/generate-certs
```

issue_title: docker.service needs to depend on the mount.
issue_body: Ran into some edge cases where the mount would not complete before docker started. This would cause docker run to never work until the docker daemon was restarted. We fixed this on our end by putting an After and Require statement in the docker.service.

issue_title: add a charm `verify-env` like command
issue_body:
Since a few environment variables are recommended to be defined during `charm-build` it may be nice to have something like `charm verify-env` to check for common settings to be available to help identify potential problems with a user's setup during build.

For example,

```
$ charm verify-env

Checking env variables

x LAYER_PATH not found
. INTERFACE_PATH found at $HOME/charms/layers
. JUJU_REPOSITORY found at $HOME/charms

Possible fixes:

echo "export LAYER_PATH=$HOME/charms/layers" >> ~/.bashrc
```

Thoughts?

issue_title: JAAS update broke controller connections
issue_body: A recent update to JAAS made it so that controllers reject calls to `RedirectInfo`.  This was apparently always the intended behavior and us calling `RedirectInfo` on a controller connection was working by accident previously.

issue_title: Default handlers log too much and there's no way to disable them through the library API
issue_body:
I'm trying to use `backoff` with a login function that connects to an external server.  The code looks something like this.

``` python
@backoff.on_exception(backoff.expo, FailedConnection)
def login(username, password):
    # stuff
```

When `on_backoff` triggers, the default handler is invoked which logs the signature which includes the password in this case.

It'd be great to have a way to disable/override the default handlers to provide my own logging behavior.

issue_title: Loss function for "mostly implicit" data
issue_body:
Hi Maciej,

I am using LightFM to train a recommender model on data from a content serving platform. The vast majority of the training data (about 9m events, 65k users, 35k items) is implicit - ie. pageviews. However, I also have a small amount of dislike data, where users could indicate that they wanted to 'See fewer posts like this'. This only makes up about 5k events, but it's obviously a pretty strong signal and I would like to include it somehow. I tried training a model with the logistic loss function and (+1/-1) scores as suggested, but it performed very poorly - presumably because we have almost no explicitly negative events. It seems to be doing better with the WARP loss function. Does it make sense at all to use WARP instead? And is there anything else you would recommend I change or try?

I am using 256 components (established through a grid search), with all other hyperparameters at their default values. Early stopping is used. 

The scores for **log loss** are as follows on the test set:
- **precision**:0.001725
- **recall:** 0.00427
- **auc**: 0.79170
- **reciprocal rank**: 0.02387

For **WARP**:
- **precision**: 0.00728
- **recall**: 0.01340
- **auc**: 0.80441
- **reciprocal rank**: 0.03838

Any feedback would be greatly appreciated. Thank you for all your work on this package!

issue_body:
Title says it all :). 

TensorFlow supports this: https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/python/tf/gather

For audio work, it's a key part of "framing" and `unsorted_segment_sum` is a key part of the inverse of framing (overlapped-add).

issue_title: pipenv install is missing an equivalent to pip's --index-url=
issue_body:
Pipfile allows for defining multiple package sources, by providing multiple named [[source]] sections.

However there's no command line way to add a new package from a specific source.

It would be convenient to be able to call something like:
```
pipenv install some-private-repo-project --index-url=https://my.repo.lan/simple
```

And have the resulting Pipfile contain:
```
[[source]]
url = "https://my.repo.lan/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "my-repo-lan"

[dev-packages]

[packages]
some-private-repo-project = {version="*",  index="my-repo-lan"}

[requires]
python_version = "2.7"
```

I've confirmed that you can create a requirements.txt file of the form:
```
--index-url https://my.repo.lan/simple
some-private-repo-project
```

and import it with:
```
pipenv install -r requirements.txt
```

and get behavior similar to the desired result, though the index isn't explicitly set on the target package.  

But it would be convenient to not have to construct that intermediate requirements.txt file first.

issue_title: ccccccgdnkiiicnhvdtvvcndcgvfjtirtcrecinfdfrf
"pip install --target" produces silently broken installation with latest setuptools
issue_body:
When installing zope.deprecation and zope.interface via `pip install --target`, a strange warning occurs and the installation is left in an incomplete state.

This is an interaction bug between PIP and Setuptools. However, since the only difference between a working and a non-working systems is the setuptools version, while the pip versions are equal, I decided to file the bug for setuptools. I tested only Python 2.7, not Python 3.

This is the simplest installation command that reproduces the bug:

```
rm -rf testdir/
pip install --target testdir/ zope.deprecation==4.2.0 zope.interface==4.4.2
```

Everything works fine on a system with latest pip 9.1.0 and **older setuptools 33.1.1**:

```
Collecting zope.deprecation==4.2.0
  Using cached zope.deprecation-4.2.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Collecting zope.interface==4.4.2
  Using cached zope.interface-4.4.2-cp27-cp27mu-manylinux1_x86_64.whl
Collecting setuptools (from zope.deprecation==4.2.0)
  Using cached setuptools-36.0.1-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Installing collected packages: setuptools, zope.deprecation, zope.interface
Successfully installed setuptools-36.0.1 zope.deprecation-4.2.0 zope.interface-4.4.2
```

However, on a system with latest pip 9.1.0 and **latest setuptools 36.0.1**, this ends with a strange warning:

```
Collecting zope.deprecation==4.2.0
  Using cached zope.deprecation-4.2.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Collecting zope.interface==4.4.2
  Using cached zope.interface-4.4.2-cp27-cp27mu-manylinux1_x86_64.whl
Collecting setuptools (from zope.deprecation==4.2.0)
  Using cached setuptools-36.0.1-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Installing collected packages: setuptools, zope.deprecation, zope.interface
Successfully installed setuptools-36.0.1 zope.deprecation-4.2.0 zope.interface-4.4.2
Target directory /home/apps/testdir/zope already exists. Specify --upgrade to force replacement.
```

Despite the warning, pip exits with a success code (exit code 0) and shows "Successfully installed" for zope.deprecation as well as zope.interface. But zope.interface is not fully installed. There is just the dist-info but not the Python package itself, i.e. the directory `interfaces/` in `testdir/zope/` is missing:

```
$ ls -1 testdir/
easy_install.py
easy_install.pyc
pkg_resources
setuptools
setuptools-36.0.1.dist-info
zope
zope.deprecation-4.2.0.dist-info
zope.deprecation-4.2.0-py2.7-nspkg.pth
zope.interface-4.4.2.dist-info
zope.interface-4.4.2-py2.7-nspkg.pth
$ ls -1 testdir/zope/
deprecation
```

Moreover, when following the advice of the warning and adding `--upgrade`, the issue is still not solved:

```
rm -rf testdir/
pip install --upgrade --target testdir/ zope.deprecation==4.2.0 zope.interface==4.4.2
```

Now, the warning vanished and pip indicates success:

```
Collecting zope.deprecation==4.2.0
  Using cached zope.deprecation-4.2.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Collecting zope.interface==4.4.2
  Using cached zope.interface-4.4.2-cp27-cp27mu-manylinux1_x86_64.whl
Collecting setuptools (from zope.deprecation==4.2.0)
  Using cached setuptools-36.0.1-py2.py3-none-any.whl
Installing collected packages: setuptools, zope.deprecation, zope.interface
Successfully installed setuptools-36.0.1 zope.deprecation-4.2.0 zope.interface-4.4.2
```

However, the situation is just flipped: zope.interface is installed but zope.deprecation is not. In other words, zope.deprecation has been replaced with zope.interfaces:

```
$ ls -1 testdir/
easy_install.py
easy_install.pyc
pkg_resources
setuptools
setuptools-36.0.1.dist-info
zope
zope.deprecation-4.2.0.dist-info
zope.deprecation-4.2.0-py2.7-nspkg.pth
zope.interface-4.4.2.dist-info
zope.interface-4.4.2-py2.7-nspkg.pth
$ ls -1 testdir/zope/
interface
```

In total, I see the following issues here:

1. `pip install --target` produces an incomplete, broken installation
   * Success is indicated despite the broken installation
   * There is just a warning, not an error message
   * The warning message is misleading, as `pip install --upgrade --target` doesn't solve the issue (even though the warning message is technically almost correct)
2. `pip install --upgrade --target` produces an incomplete, broken installation
   * Success is indicated despite the broken installation
   * There is neither a warning nor an error message

issue_title: Change default sampling rate for tracing to 1%
issue_body:
The current default sampling rate of 100% is unheard of in distributed tracing. If deployed in a high-throughput service it can easily DDOS the tracing system.

https://github.com/uber/tchannel-python/blob/master/tchannel/zipkin/zipkin_trace.py#L32
```python
    DEFAULT_RATE = 1.0
```

issue_title: Validation error on account name & account key parameters
issue_body:
Set account name but not key.
```
$ export AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT=mystorageaccount
```
After running `$ az storage blob list -c vhds`, I expect an error saying storage key not specified or similar but instead, I get the following.

```
$ az storage blob list -c vhds
usage: az storage blob list [-h] [--output {json,tsv,list,table,jsonc}]
                            [--verbose] [--debug] [--query JMESPATH]
                            --container-name CONTAINER_NAME
                            [--timeout TIMEOUT] [--account-name ACCOUNT_NAME]
                            [--marker MARKER] [--sas-token SAS_TOKEN]
                            [--connection-string CONNECTION_STRING]
                            [--num-results NUM_RESULTS] [--include INCLUDE]
                            [--delimiter DELIMITER]
                            [--account-key ACCOUNT_KEY] [--prefix PREFIX]
az storage blob list: error: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'id'
```

The bug is [in _validators.py](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/master/src/command_modules/azure-cli-storage/azure/cli/command_modules/storage/_validators.py#L46), `acc_id = next((x for x in scf.storage_accounts.list() if x.name == n.account_name), None).id`.

If n.account_name isn't in the storage accounts list, the iterator will return None and so `'NoneType' object has no attribute 'id'`

issue_title: Enable paging in command results
issue_body:
Today, for commands which produce large amount of result data and take long time to execute the user experience is poor. The command doesn't provide feedback to indicate progress. The final output can be huge and inconvenient to consume if it is not captured in file.

Many REST API our commands depend on actually provide server side and/or client side paging. By leveraging the paging feature, we can provide a friendlier user interaction. If user opt in, the command can print result piece by piece as each page arrives.

The goal of this task is to design and implement such a mechanism in the core of the CLI so that most of the commands can be equipped with paging feature without changes to the commands themselves as long as the underlying Azure API supports paging.

issue_body:
The `InstallAzureCli` installer doesn't work except when the user can provide terminal input via a Mac/Linux tty device.    Even if you type `yes '' | bash InstallAzureCli` after downloading the script, it doesn't work because it insists on the tty device.

The use of a tty is quite unnecessary given there are no passwords or input line editing features involved here.

This is important for use with Ansible, Chef, Puppet, Docker (building your own image), etc. My application is to ensure that a server used to run Azure CLI commands can be re-provisioned entirely by Ansible.

I'm aware of the bundled installer (#3376), but that is now quite out of date (2.0.10 vs current 2.0.20) and I need a recent version of azure-cli to fix a concurrency bug with `clouds.config`.  Same applies to APT packages.

Please can you provide a command line option such as `bash InstallAzureCli --non-interactive` so that this works?  Or just enable `yes '' | bash InstallAzureCli` by reading from standard input instead of a tty?

Or alternatively, build the bundled installer for every Azure CLI release.

Many other tools such as `dpkg` provide such options.   I'm not aware of a workaround, except perhaps for running the installer under `expect` which is quite a hassle for a CLI tool.

---

### Environment summary

Install Method (e.g. pip, interactive script, apt-get, Docker, MSI, edge build) / CLI version (`az --version`) / OS version / Shell Type (e.g. bash, cmd.exe, Bash on Windows)  

```
InstallAzureCli installer, under Ansible, on macOS 10.11 and Ubuntu 16.04.

Azure CLI version: azure-cli (2.0.20) - and earlier versions

```

issue_title: [Backup] Suggest: `backup policy create-for-vm`
issue_body:
Looking at the structure of the backup policy object, it seems like a strongly typed create/update command could be created for this scenario.

There are no collections of complex objects, which is typically the reason that JSON objects are used. As far as I can tell, there are only single (albeit polymorphic) objects and some simple collections that could easily be handled with `nargs='+'`.

A true create/update experience would be preferable to a JSON object set experience as policies could be configured in the script rather than JSON files needing to be saved as artifacts. It would also allow for simple updating of existing policies without have to download, modify, save and re-set it.

---

issue_title: Error when executing ingesting ingestion of multiple files
issue_body:
Receive the following error when attempting to process multiple local csv files:

`Bad request: Request is invalid and cannot be executed. Entity ID '[DB NetDefaultDB v?.?]' of kind 'Database' was not found.`

I'm passing my DB name as the database I'm connecting to so I searched the azure-kusto-ingest source and found that the first place that NetDefaultDB is used in the process is the _get_temp_storage_objects function:

```
def _get_temp_storage_objects(self):
        response = self._kusto_client.execute_mgmt("NetDefaultDB", ".create tempstorage")
        storages = list()
        for row in response.iter_all():
            storages.append(_ConnectionString.parse(row["StorageRoot"]))
        return storages
```

The execute management function first variable is the DB. Since the "NetDefaultDB" is a string I'm not sure if this is this part of Kusto's inner working for ingestion and this error is permission related or if this value is supposed to be a variable related to the DB actually being accessed.

issue_title: Can't create vm with exsiting vm image + fix found work for me
issue_body:
Bug found: when I use create_virtual_machine_deployment to  create a new virtual machine with a existing vm image, i got this 

`<bad request : No Configuration Set should be specified while using a VMImage with a specialized OS Disk Configuration>`

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Corrige found in: `azure/servicemanangement/__init__.py`
in function role_to_xml,
`if system_configuration_set or network_configuration_set:`

should be changed to
`if (system_configuration_set or network_configuration_set) and not vm_image_name:`


Else it will add `<ConfigurationSets>` tag and the api server will refuse this request.

Please fixed this in the next update, thankyou!

issue_title: Accounts parent_id in records table not being set correctly
issue_body:
Ubuntu 16.04
From source Latest commit 5e2a13b
Postgres database

1. Change kinto.ini to enable accounts
2. Create admin user myadmin with
`kinto.account_create_principals = system.Everyone
`
3. Then change to
`kinto.account_create_principals = account:myadmin`

Final kinto.ini changes
```
multiauth.policies = account
multiauth.policy.account.use = kinto.plugins.accounts.authentication.AccountsAuthenticationPolicy
# Allow anyone to create accounts.
kinto.account_create_principals = account:myadmin
# Set user 'account:admin' as the administrator.
kinto.account_write_principals = account:myadmin
kinto.account_read_principals = account:myadmin

```

4.  Create user using myadmin auth
echo '{"data": {"id": "zzz", "password": "test"}}' | http POST http://192.168.2.12:8888/v1/accounts --auth 'myadmin:test'

5. Try to delete user
`http DELETE http://192.168.2.12:8888/v1/accounts/zzz --verbose --auth 'myadmin:test'`

```
{
    "code": 404,
    "details": {
        "id": "zzz",
        "resource_name": "account"
    },
    "errno": 110,
    "error": "Not Found"
}
```

6. If you change user zzz's password then Delete is a success
echo '{"data": {"password": "asdf"}}' | http PUT http://localhost:8888/v1/accounts/zzz --verbose --auth 'myadmin:test'

If you look at the records table in Postgres you will see that the initial creation of user zzz's parent_id is set to *

After changing zzz's password parent_id is set to zzz

issue_title: Preprocessor doesn't differentiate between VUnit check() and other procedures of the same name
issue_body:
The VUnit pre-processor adds line number and filename arguments to the check() procedure calls. Unfortunately, it doesn't discriminate between VUnit's check() procedures and any other procedure / function of the same name, so it adds these parameters to all such procedures. This then causes compilation failures.
The same happens with log(), which in my case clashes with a function to calculate logarithms.

issue_title: conda.common.signals.signal_handler throwing exceptions on travis
issue_body:
The conda.common.signals.signal_handler context manager [introduced in 4.3.14](https://github.com/conda/conda/compare/4.3.13...4.3.14#diff-1ce3aba0f5b5277dc9830e6671cab0f1R35) is causing my travis conda build to fail in the midst of a `conda build` command, throwing `TypeError`s - see [this travis build](https://travis-ci.org/ipython-contrib/jupyter_contrib_nbextensions/jobs/220349775#L633-L662) for a failed example, and then [this build using 4.3.13](https://travis-ci.org/ipython-contrib/jupyter_contrib_nbextensions/jobs/220359572) which does exactly the same with the earlier version which still works.

These builds still work fine for me locally (Ubuntu 16.04), so I assume this is something related to Travis (currenlty apparently using Ubuntu 12.04 or 14.04) somehow, although I'm not sure what...

issue_title: CDT metadata discussion
issue_body:
Prototype (not to be followed) for CI system is at:

https://github.com/msarahan/ci_test

Pay special attention to:
- build_platforms.d
- test_platforms.d
- versions.yml

These don't implement everything we need.  In particular, they don't really directly capture things like glibc version.  versions.yml is currently limited solely to the few packages that conda-build has hard-coded.  It really should support arbitrary packages.  This ties into https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/1142

issue_title: Use python-progressbar under the BSD license?
issue_body: The python-progressbar package is [dual licensed under both LGPL the BSD licesnses](https://github.com/niltonvolpato/python-progressbar/blob/master/LICENSE.txt), including [back to the commit](https://github.com/niltonvolpato/python-progressbar/tree/cc8d9ebd1a9f43eb4d80297d2e6435748c053457) that looks to correspond to the version initially imported into Conda. To be consistent with the overall license of conda, can we switch to using the code under the terms of the BSD license?

issue_title: missing rpath for libmkl_intel_thread?
issue_body:
In Julia, which loads `libpython` as a shared library (installed via conda by default), we are suddenly (with a recent conda upgrade) getting MKL failures on MacOS because it can't find `libmkl_intel_thread.dylib`.     Manually setting `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` to Conda's `usr/lib` directory (where that library is found) fixes the problem, but that didn't used to be required, and isn't required if I run the `python` executable directly.

My guess is that there is a missing shared-library dependency somewhere — something should have explicitly linked `-lmkl_intel_thread` but didn't do so?  Or maybe a missing `-rpath` argument?

For example, we get this when plotting via matplotlib (JuliaPy/PyPlot.jl#315), but also when calling any numpy linear-algebra function:
```
julia> using PyCall

julia> pyimport("numpy.linalg")["inv"](rand(100,100))
Intel MKL FATAL ERROR: Cannot load libmkl_intel_thread.dylib.
```
(This just calls `numpy.linalg.inv` on a random 100x100 numpy matrix.  Under the hood, it is calling `PyImport_ImportModule("numpy.linalg")` in `libpython` etcetera, using the standard C embedding API.)

issue_title: conda build causes different compiler flags being used
issue_body:
I try to compile pymc on OSX. If I just run `python setup.py` build it works fine, but `conda build` of the same package produces an error. The `build.sh` does nothing more: https://github.com/fonnesbeck/conda-recipes/blob/master/pymc/build.sh

I tracked it down to different compile flags being set, manually calling `setup.py` produces gfortran being called with these flags, among others:

`/usr/bin/gfortran -Wall -m64 -Wall -undefined dynamic_lookup -bundle`

While conda build which also just calls setup.py omits some necessary flags:
`/usr/bin/gfortran -Wall -arch x86_64`

Why would the flags be different between these two?

issue_title: missing windows magic
issue_body:
I gave `conda` a try for testing on windows.  I found that with the install of miniconda 3.10.1 (64-bit), there were a few things that had to be manually patched so stuff worked as expected.

If you want to run python from the command line:
- associate .py file with conda's python.exe

Not sure if `conda` could or should assume that's what you want.  I think probably.

The bigger issue is that there was some command line magic missing from the registry keys.
These two registry entries needed editing with regedit:
- HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\Applications\python27.exe\shell\open\command
- HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\py_auto_file\shell\open\command

Each key needed a `%*` to be added to the end of the existing command string -- otherwise conda python ignores all but the first command line argument.

issue_title: S3: allow configuring boto3
issue_body:
It would be nice if it was possible to configure S3 adapter (which uses boto3) internally. An example use case is using non-AWS S3 services (like DigitalOcean or many others), in which case you have
to specify `endpoint_url` which has to be passed to `boto3` directly so there's no way of storing it in a standard config file or environment variable.

Even if it's just being able to provide `endpoint_url` for S3 somewhere directly, it would help greatly.

issue_title: Preview presets
issue_body:
Predefined presets with tasks to deploy previews to make it easier to setup and mantain preview configuration for standard projects.

Should have a list of tasks and the "daemon task" and run preview tasks defined in frigg config after the preset tasks before running the daemon task.

Example 

```yaml
preview:
  preset: django
  tasks:
   - python manage.py loaddata fixtures/preview.json
```

issue_title: How difficult would it be to expand the client to support changing a user's password?
issue_body:
I noticed changing a user's password is not currently supported. Doing so returns the error "Password modification is not yet supported", per this code:

https://github.com/irods/python-irodsclient/blob/master/irods/manager/user_manager.py#L61-L62

Is there something complex about that command, that requires intentionally looking for it and returning an error, while the other commands fall through to the `GeneralAdminRequest`?

I'm asking because I'd like to use the `python-irodsclient` in a Django server, but changing a user's password is one of the critical commands I need, for which the lack of support is currently preventing me from using the client. The interim approach I'm following involves using Python's `subprocess` module to invoke `iCommands` directly or invoke scripts that invoke `iCommands`. Admittedly not a great solution, but working for the moment.

I'd be willing to submit a PR to add support for changing a user's password, if you can provide guidance on why it's not currently implemented, and how difficult it might be for me to include it, or maybe some pointers on what I should be aware of in attempting to do so.

Is that something you'd be willing to advise on?

2. LICENSE doesn't need 'license'

issue_title: Keras Support
issue_body: Since Keras can use Tensorflow as the backend, how can one use TensorFlowOnSpark with Keras if it is possible at all? Thank you.

issue_title: Change camera settings
issue_body: A programmatic way to configure how to grab the frames from BeamNG (camera position, FOV, etc) would be ideal.

issue_title: [build]  OSX link error due: wanting to link -lboost_python-py27
issue_body: Probably introduced by commit 83e70c2. On OSX, with homebrew it seems the py2 boost python library is still called `libboost_python.dylib`.

issue_title: Canopy Python issues
issue_body:
I just tried to install Canopy with Python 3.  I ran into lots of problems, and wasn't ultimately able to set it as my default Python.  I submitted a bug report, but I am increasingly convinced that we should stick with recommending Anaconda, since installing Canopy seems to be such a mess.  Another issue with Canopy is that they are relatively slow with updating packages, which Anaconda does significantly faster.

The only problem with Anaconda is the Framework build of python screen issue, but at this point I think the benefits outweigh the downsides.

issue_title: add_jinja2_renderer `name` only works for default search path
issue_body:
If I add a jinja2 renderer into my Pyramid application looking for `.j2` files, it will only find files in the default search path.  For example, if `mypackage/__init__.py` adds the renderer, only the directory `mypackage/templates` is searched.

```
config.add_jinja2_renderer('.j2')
config.add_jinja2_search_path('anotherpackage:templates/')
```
In this case `.j2` files in another package will not be found because the `name='.jinja2'` by default on this function.

**Expected Behavior**

That `add_jinja2_renderer` would "register" into the renderer system that `.j2` files should always be rendered using Jinja2, regardless of the package / search path.

The state of the `.j2` registration would be captured and used in other functions/methods.

**Actual Behavior**

The `add_jinja2_renderer` function / method only registers `.j2` files for the default search path, any additional search paths need to pass the extension name.

Work around:

```
config.add_jinja2_renderer('.j2')
config.add_jinja2_search_path('anotherpackage:templates/', name='.j2')
```

issue_title: Missing order of setpoints in the new dataset
issue_body:
Currently the retrieval of setpoint data relies on the order of the registered dependent paramenters. So for example the code below will fail for `depends_on_wrong` and pass for `depends_on_right`

```
import qcodes as qc
from qcodes import ParamSpec, new_data_set, new_experiment
from qcodes.dataset.database import initialise_database
from qcodes.dataset.data_export import get_data_by_id

initialise_database()
new_experiment('issue1239', 'test2')

indep1 = ParamSpec('indep1', "numeric")
indep2 = ParamSpec('indep2', "numeric")
depends_on_right = [indep1, indep2]
depends_on_wrong = [indep2, indep1]
dep = ParamSpec('response', "numeric", depends_on=depends_on_wrong)

dataset = new_data_set("results", specs=[indep1, indep2, dep])
dataset.add_result({'response': 1234,
                    'indep2': 2,
                    'indep1': 1 })
dataset.mark_complete()

d = get_data_by_id(dataset.run_id)
# test if data is correctly saved
for el in d[0]:
    if el['name'] == 'indep2':
        val = el['data']
        break
# val[0] is actually 1, i.e indep2 and indep1 are interchanged
assert val[0] == 2
```

In the failing case the values for the setpoint axes get interchanged, because the array of dependent ParamSpecs is reversed. 

This behavior is of course undesired and has to be resolved. 
In my opinion this problem roots all the way back to the `get_setpoints` method of `sqlite_base.py`, which returns the setpoints as a `List[List[List[Any]]]`, for a parameter specified by its name. I don't think that we should establish some standard order of the setpoints to resolve this problem. We should possibly rather return a dictionary with the setpoint parameter names as keys instead of one level of the lists.


I will start identifying the uses of the named function and thereafter start implementing a fix that involves a dictionary for this purpose.

issue_title: Slow parsing
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm running performance tests with complete graphs (containing all possible triples for a given set of resources), but parsing is taking too long. I've found [this similar issue](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/pull/318), but it talks about serialization. In my case, the data is in memory.

This is the function which generates the complete graph. `data` is just a string in turtle format containing the triples.
```
def full_database(n, prefix='%s'):
    data = ''
    for i in range(1,n+1):
        for j in range(1,n+1):
            for k in range(1,n+1):
                data += '<'+(prefix%i) +'> <'+ (prefix%j) +'> <'+ \
                (prefix%k) + '> .\n'
    return data
```
This is the main code, which measures time.
```
if __name__ == '__main__':
    D = Graph()
    size = int(sys.argv[1])
    start = time.time()
    D.parse(data=full_database(size), format="n3")
    end = time.time()
    
    print size, end-start
```
And the results were:
```
size    triples   time
   0          0     0.0100710391998
  10       1000     0.644206047058
  20       8000     5.14649105072
  30      27000    17.3461780548

  70     343000   218.672528982
  80     512000   326.651834011
  90     729000   462.141964912
```
The amount of triples is `size`^3. 

Is this time normal for this input?

issue_title: [Call for Contribution] Image augmenter operators
issue_body:
# Goal 
Fuse operations like (x - mean)/std into a single operator in backend to speed up IO.

# References

- https://github.com/pytorch/vision/blob/master/torchvision/transforms/transforms.py
- https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/python/mxnet/image/image.py
- https://github.com/huangzehao/incubator-mxnet-bk/blob/master/src/io/image_aug_default.cc#L240
https://www.tensorflow.org/api_guides/python/image#Image_Adjustments

# Operators

## mx.nd/sym/image.resized_crop(data, box, size, interp) -> out
- data: H x W x C uint8 tensor
- box: tuple of 4 integers (y, x, h, w)
- size: size integer or (height, width) tuple
- interp: default bilinear
- out: cropped image tensor

## mx.nd/sym.image.flip(data, axis) -> out
- data: H x W x C uint8 tensor
- axis: 0 or 1, axis along which to flip
- out: C x H x W tensor
Note: needs to support inplace operation.

## mx.nd/sym.image.to_tensor(data) -> out
- data: H x W x C uint8 tensor
- out: C x H x W float32 tensor

## mx.nd/sym.image.normalize(data, mean, std) -> out
- data: C x H x W tensor
- mean: tuple of 1 or 3 floats
- std: tuple of 1 or 3 floats

## mx.nd/sym.image.random_brightness(data, max_brightness) -> out
- data: C x H x W tensor
- max_brightness: float

## mx.nd/sym.image.random_contrast(data, max_contrast) -> out
- data: C x H x W tensor
- max_contrast: float

## mx.nd/sym.image.random_saturation(data, max_saturation) -> out
- data: C x H x W tensor
- max_saturation: float

## mx.nd/sym.image.random_hue(data, max_hue) -> out
- data: C x H x W tensor
- max_hue: float

## mx.nd/sym.image.random_color_jitter(data, max_brightness, max_contrast, max_saturation, max_hue) -> out
Apply 4 augs in random order

## mx.nd/sym.image.random_lighting(data, alphastd, eig) -> out
- data: C x H x W tensor
- alphastd: float
- eig: tuple of 9 floats (might be better to use ndarray? not sure...)

issue_title: from_logits definition seems different from what is expected?
issue_body:
In loss functions like SoftmaxCrossEntropy, the definition of from_logits is:
from_logits (bool, default False) – Whether input is a log probability (usually from log_softmax) instead of unnormalized numbers.

It seems mxnet calls the output of a log_softmax a logit, whereas others call (any) unscaled log probabilities logits.

e.g. In tensorflow/keras api:
e.g. https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/python/tf/nn/softmax_cross_entropy_with_logits_v2
from_logits: Boolean, whether output is the result of a softmax, or is a tensor of logits.

I think mxnet is consistent with the definition of logits:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Logit

But highlighting the difference may be useful in general. I had a bit of trouble converting a model from Keras to Gluon because of this.

issue_title: Semi-formalize style with automated formatting support

I realize Assimp doesn't have a hard and fast style guide, but it might be nice to formalize some of the existing style patterns in a script/config that can be run by contributors locally to maintain a consistent look and feel.

If we create and host a .clang-format style configuration file, other developers (and or maintainers) could hook into that quite easily (see, for example, the [ClangFormat Sublime Text package](https://sublime.wbond.net/packages/Clang%20Format)). issue_title: Refectorize how items are fetched from Perceval
issue_body:
The current implementation for fetching items from `perceval` backends is messy. It uses `BackendCommand` to parse the arguments and calls directly to `fetch` and `fetch_from_cache` methods of 'Backend' class. See:

https://github.com/chaoss/grimoirelab-elk/blob/master/grimoire_elk/arthur.py#L119
https://github.com/chaoss/grimoirelab-elk/blob/master/grimoire_elk/ocean/elastic.py#L154

There are better ways to support this case. For instance, using only `BackendCommand` instances or calling to `backend.fetch` or `backend.fetch_from_archive` functions.

The current implementation is also broken for Perceval >= 0.9.11 (see #222).
Issue #224 is also related to this task.

CSRF verification failed. Request aborted.

Help
Reason given for failure:

    CSRF token missing or incorrect.
    
In general, this can occur when there is a genuine Cross Site Request Forgery, or when Django's CSRF mechanism has not been used correctly. For POST forms, you need to ensure:

Your browser is accepting cookies.
The view function passes a request to the template's render method.
In the template, there is a {% csrf_token %} template tag inside each POST form that targets an internal URL.
If you are not using CsrfViewMiddleware, then you must use csrf_protect on any views that use the csrf_token template tag, as well as those that accept the POST data.
The form has a valid CSRF token. After logging in in another browser tab or hitting the back button after a login, you may need to reload the page with the form, because the token is rotated after a login.
You're seeing the help section of this page because you have DEBUG = True in your Django settings file. Change that to False, and only the initial error message will be displayed.

You can customize this page using the CSRF_FAILURE_VIEW setting.

issue_title: Respect output format for error presentation whenever possible
issue_body:
@bd4 I want us to discuss this a little bit.

I think it makes a lot of sense that `globus transfer ls --format json --endpoint-id 'badepid'` would produce a JSON error message.

However, there are a number of conditions under which we do not receive JSON from the other end of the connection.
For example, the web server may respond with a raw body of `500 Internal Server Error`.
In those cases, should we wrap this in a standard JSON container, like `{"message": "500 Internal Server Error"}` ?

To me, the target use case is stuff like nonexistent endpoints, which the caller may want to parse and inspect.
They should be able to, _relatively_ safely, hand stderr to something that consumes JSON.

I think that would be totally acceptable, but I'm not sure I've thought through all of the options.
There are errors that we can't handle so easily in this way, namely Python stacktraces.
I don't want to be JSONifying every exception ever thrown, just to slavishly obey the request for `--format JSON`

Where do we draw the line? Do we say "We will convert any error responses from the APIs to JSON." ?
If we can't crystalize this into a simple statement, we probably don't have clear enough thinking about it yet.

issue_title: Brotli compression ratio depends on endianness of CPU
issue_body:
TL;DR: Just realized that "unaligned load + mask" technique used by some hashers will give endianness-inconsistent results.

This problem does not affect correctness, only what backward references selection and, consequently, compression ratio.

Lets say, hasher depends on `5` bytes of input. Then, it does the following: `hash = magic * (load64(src) & 0xFFFFFFFFFF)`. On little-endian CPU the first `5` bytes of `src` will affect the resulting hash. On big-endian CPU, the first `3` bytes will be ignored, and next `5` bytes will be taken into account. This way, on big-endian CPU, matches of length `[5..7]` are likely to be missed by hasher. On the other side there will be more `8+` matches.

issue_title: Option for custom duplicate key handling
issue_body:
This is more a question then an issue: There is the `json` option, which allows for duplicate keys. It changes the regular behaviour to overwrite previously defined keys instead of throwing an error.

I would like to discuss/propose a change that would extend the `json` option – or maybe better yet introduce a new option – to define a function that allows for a custom handling of duplicate keys.
Use case: The `json` option overwrites keys completely and one would like to deep merge the existing structure with a partial one, which is the value of the duplicate key.

This logic could be implemented with a check for an already defined `_result[keyNode]` here:
https://github.com/nodeca/js-yaml/blob/master/lib/js-yaml/loader.js#L309
It would then call the function provided via the option with `_result[keyNode]` (current value) and `valueNode` (new value, in the described use case the one that gets merged into the current value) and return the merged result.

My intent is to discuss the idea and possible implications of it, which I might not be aware of. Thanks! :)

issue_title: Cannot reproduce Breakout benchmark using Double DQN
issue_body:
I haven't been able to reproduce the results of the Breakout benchmark with Double DQN when using similar hyperparameter values than the ones presented in the original paper. After more than 20M observed frames (~100.000 episodes), the mean 100 episode reward still remains around 10, having achieved a maximum value of 12. 

I present in the following list the neural network configuration as well as the hyperparameter values that I'm using in case I'm missing or getting something important wrong:
```
env = gym.make("BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4")
env = ScaledFloatFrame(wrap_dqn(env))
model = deepq.models.cnn_to_mlp(
        convs=[(32, 8, 4), (64, 4, 2), (64, 3, 1)],
        hiddens=[512],
        dueling=False
)
act = deepq.learn(
        env,
        q_func=model,
        lr=25e-5,
        max_timesteps=200000000,
        buffer_size=100000, #cannot store 1M frames as the paper suggests
        exploration_fraction=1000000/float(200000000), #so as to finish after !M steps
        exploration_final_eps=0.1,
        train_freq=4,
        batch_size=32,
        learning_starts=50000,
        target_network_update_freq=10000,
        gamma=0.99,
        prioritized_replay=False
)
```

Does anyone have some idea of what is going wrong? The analogous results exposed in a [jupyter notebook in `openai/baselines-results`](https://github.com/openai/baselines-results/blob/master/dqn_results.ipynb) indicates that I should be able to get much better scores.

Thanks in advance.

issue_title: Return type that is implemented in another library with another set of python bindings
issue_body:
Hi,

I am trying to use this project to make python bindings to my library. I do, however, use another library, which already have a set of python bindings. I would then like to return an object from the 3rd party library in my python bindings. Perhaps a little example is justified:

In my library I have something like
```c++
TVector3 getCenter(){...} // TVector3 is 3rd party library type
```
This 3rd party already have a set of python bindings:
```python
import ROOT
v = ROOT.TVector3() # 3rd party object
v.Print() # 3rd party method
```

What I would like is now to write python bindings to my method, which returns an object from the already-implemented set of python bindings such that I can do something like this:

```python
import MyLib

v = MyLib.getCenter() # My library returning a 3rd party object
v.Print() # 3rd party method
```

I have tried to poke around in your section about custom type casts (http://pybind11.readthedocs.io/en/latest/advanced/cast/custom.html) and also the `chrono.h` and `eigen.h` to get some inspiration, but I can't seem to find a proper way of doing this. I am a bit confused about lines like
```c++
return PyLong_FromLong(src.long_value)
```
and how I can construct a method like that for my problem. 

Is this even feasible?

issue_title: Build failure with Boost 1.53 and boost::variant
Bizarre Python 3.x failure when creating method aliases
patsy question/wishlist
issue_body:
Hello,

I fit some (relatively) large-ish GLMs in statsmodels and have been experimenting with using `patsy` instead of a home rolled thing. My home rolled method isn't very good (I tend to underestimate challenges...). I've gotten some better hardware so now some models that used to not work with patsy (because of memory constraints) work now. I've run across a few things that might make it easier for me to use patsy more. Happy to work on PRs for them if there's interest. 

1. Categorical NA logic: Currently, it appears that when a categorical is fed through `patsy`, every inidividual value is checked against a rather detailed list on how to handle NaNs/Missing Values/Empty whatever. I ran a cProfile on this and it was quite slow. I think the bottleneck is here:
https://github.com/pydata/patsy/blob/master/patsy/categorical.py#L341
I know that NaNs/NA/None/Empty is a mess in general, but its a fact of life in my line of work (insurance modeling). I'm wondering if we scope out exactly all the scenarios we need to control for and use (pandas maybe?) to do this more elegantly? I'm not sure of the scope as there's far more players here than me.

2. Reading from on-disk data stores: Memory is something of a problem for me. a typical model that I run might eat up 10+ GB of RAM. It works, but obviously is not ideal. As far as (relatively) mature tools, I've found bcolz's ctables to be pretty good (and fast). HDFStores/dask would be nice too. I'm not sure if #91 relates to this (I don't know if xarray works well as an on-disk storage/data tool). 

3. Partial predictions: This is partly a statsmodels partly a patsy idea... I'd like the ability to do a so-called partial predict. Essentially I have a model like `y ~ a + b + a:c`. I want to come up with predictions `y` assuming that just `a` changes or just `b` changes. I think the process would look something like (assuming we're talking about changing only `a`) creating a new design matrix with every unique value of `a` as a separate row, and have the most frequent (or some other innocuous value) of `b` and `c` as constant for these rows. Then feed this dataset through the statsmodels `predict` routine. This is very helpful for GLMs with the log link--which is the bulk of what I work with.

issue_title: Integer expressions should evaluate to integers
Eclipse errors and warnings when importing DICOM set, generated from TRiP files format 1.4
issue_body:
![error_jet03](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/22157203/23257784/9e3d95aa-f9c5-11e6-8844-6f4458f16b1f.png)
I was trying to test different formats of TRiP files (.vdx,.hed,.ctx) being converted into DICOM using pytrip 1.3.1 in relation to #245. 
I had some files in format 1.4 originally converted from DICOM into TRiP format around two years ago using the then-version of pytrip. When I converted these back into DICOM and imported them in Eclipse, I got the above shown errors and warnings and the import failed (there are more warnings not seen in the screen window but they are identical to the last couple of warnings). It is the first time I see these errors. Normally we just get some warnings and the import works.

To recap, the difference between the files tested until now and the warnings/errors they give: 
1) Niels' are generated from TRiP format 2.0. Where they originally DICOM? No warnings, no errors, import works.
2) Alinas files are generated from TRiP format 1.2 from DICOM originally converted into TRiP format using dcm2trip. Some warnings #255, no errors, import works.    
3) In this case files are generated from TRiP format 1.4 from DICOM originally converted into TRiP format using an older 2015 version of pytripgui. We get warnings, errors and the import fails. 
An important note is that the original DICOM Alina uses and the ones I use are from the same place. Only the TRiP format (and the way they were made into this format) are different.

Now it remains to be figured out if this issue comes from the difference in format or if the older version of pytrip had bugs when generating TRiP files?

issue_title: Fix CSS for images on homepage
Project name sanitization
issue_body:
As described [here](http://www.spect.cl/blog/2015/11/security-audit-scrapyd/) (point 1,2,3 and 5) there are some security issues related to **project name**. I've thought in a fix sanitizing the project name value using the same logic as with variable `version`:

```
re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]', '_', version)
```

It would have to apply to every method working with project name in `FilesystemEggStorage` to have consistency adding and then getting projects. It adds as side efect that two projects with non-common characters like `project!` and `project?` will share the same project name `project_`. Does someone see a better solution?

issue_title: Accent-insensitive LIKE
issue_body:
Hi all--
we have several use-cases where we need a LIKE query to ignore accents (e.g. matching "a" and "á"). Is this possible already, or can it be made possible?

PostgreSQL (which all of these cases use) provides the unaccent-extension, so that I thought it might be useful to expose that, either as unaccent() operator for Expressions, or as an extension to the .like() operator as in: Field.like(..., ignore_accents=True).

Surely, unaccent needs to be switched on by "CREATE EXTENSION unaccent", which could happen e.g. during adapter instantiation, parametrized as unaccent=True.

How complicated would that be? How can it be done? Anyone who can assist, or give the right pointers?

Many thanks,
Dominic

P.S. Alternative to use REGEX already considered, and actually working, but not scalable enough.

issue_title: ssh.select_random_ports keeps increasing size of _random_ports forever
issue_body:
I have an application that periodically calls ssh.tunnel_connection in a long running service and after a few days will hang due to a high CPU load on the threads that call tunnel_connection. I've traced the code and noticed that for every call to ssh.tunnel_connection the internal select_random_ports call adds a new socket to the internal set, _random_ports. Eventually I've seen that the set (python-2.7.9-14.1.x86_64) tops out at size 28228 and the calling threads go into a high CPU spin. I never see the set size decreasing nor where the list of unused local ports is cleaned up.

I'm currently using python-pyzmq-14.0.0-2.13.x86_64 distributed via SLES12 but I just checked the code in master and the problem seems to still be in master. 

I'd also like to know if the pyzmq library is thread safe.

The problem is easy to reproduce by either calling select_random_ports in a loop (one or more threads to speed things up) or calling the exported tunnel_connection to open a tunnel over a longer period of time.

issue_title: Magic for functions
issue_body:
One of the limitations of MATLAB is that you can't define functions in the REPL, they must be in a separate file.  This seems to carry over to this kernel:

    function a=test()
       disp('test')
    end

Results in:

    Error: Function definitions are not permitted in this context.

It would be nice if there was a magic for functions that would automatically save the function with the same name defined by the function and put the directory the file is in the path.

I know this can be done manually using %%file magic and then manually adding the current directory (or whatever directory you use) to the session's path, but this seems to be such a basic feature I think it warrants a dedicated macro (I know the lack of the feature is primarily MATLAB's fault).

A possible call signature would be as follows:

    %%function [-t|--directory==DIRECTORY|--parent==PARENT]

`-t` would save the file to a temporary directory (defined by pythons' `tempfile` module), `--directory` would save the file to `DIRECTORY`, and `--parent` would store the file as a subfunction of parent function `PARENT`.  These options are mutually exclusive.  I think the default directory would be a subdirectory of the current directory, perhaps `functions`.

issue_title: Storing models and parameters, 'picklability'
issue_body:
It should be possible to easily store trained models. There are two ways to do this:

1) store the parameter values. This is typically what I do myself. The actual architecture of the model is not stured, but the definition of the model architecture is just a Python file, and that is where the architecture is 'stored', as far as I'm concerned. So I'd do something like this:
```
l_out = ... # output layer of the network
all_params = nntools.layers.get_all_params(l_out)
all_param_values = [p.get_value() for p in all_params]
store(all_param_values, ...)
```
where `store` is a placeholder for pickling or something to that extent.

To load this model again:
```
all_param_values = load(...)
all_params = nntools.layers.get_all_params(l_out)
for p, v in zip(all_params, all_param_values):
    p.set_value(v)
```

This works because get_all_params always gives you the parameter variables in the same order. We should probably provide two utility functions for this.

2) pickle the entire model, parameters included (i.e. `l_out`). I personally don't do this usually, but it's kind of the natural thing to do in Python and I believe a lot of people will try this and expect it to work. So I think we should support this approach.

This imposes some limitations on what language features we can use. For example, lambdas are not 'picklable', and functions aren't either unless they are defined in the top level of a module (see https://docs.python.org/2/library/pickle.html#what-can-be-pickled-and-unpickled ). So some functional programming patterns cannot be used if we want to keep everything picklable.

That's unfortunate, but I think it is a good idea to keep picklability in mind nevertheless. What does everyone think?

I still think we should support the approach of storing only the parameter values as well, though.

issue_title: add batch-wise dropout layer, leaky ReLUs, ...
issue_body:
The results of the CIFAR-10 competition on Kaggle are out, and the 1st ranked solution uses some interesting tricks that we should probably make available in nntools: http://www.kaggle.com/c/cifar-10/forums/t/10493/train-you-very-own-deep-convolutional-network

**leaky ReLUs**
This idea has been proposed before, but I haven't really seen it used in the wild until now. Instead of having `y = max(0, x)`, you have `y = max(x/a, x)`, where a is some constant (say `a = 10` or `a = 3`). This means you still get some sort of nonlinearity, but the gradient can flow through in both regimes. I don't think we have a clean way to do parameterised nonlinearities yet, maybe we need to create a callable class for this, like we have for the initializers. We could just use nested functions, but that will cause issues with picklability of the models (see #7).

**batch-wise dropout**
Instead of sampling a random dropout mask for each sample, and multiplying the batch with the mask, sample only one dropout mask for the entire batch and index the batch and parameter matrices to completely delete all dropped out units. Between two layers with batch-wise dropout, the size of the dot product is reduced by 75%, so this could mean a speedup of up to 4x.

Of course this will also affect the learning trajectory, but I suspect it has advantages and disadvantages (it's probably not inherently worse than doing sample-wise dropout). I don't know if it will be feasible to reap the gains in computational efficiency in Theano, since implementing this will require advanced indexing (i.e. making copies). But it's worth implementing and evaluating, it doesn't sound too hard.

Unfortunately it probably isn't possible to isolate this into a separate layer like we did with regular dropout, since the parameter matrices have be to be indexed with the dropout mask.

issue_title: Name layers created using batch_norm() utility
issue_body:
This is a _very_ minor issue, but I think it would be nice for layers created using the `batch_norm()` utility to be named. This way all layers will be named when e.g. printing out network structures.

The created `BatchNormLayer` and `NonlinearityLayer` layers could be named by appending a suffix to the name of the wrapped layer, separated by a dot (like in the recurrent layers). In case the `kwargs` argument passed on to the BN layer already contains a name, it could be used to name the `BatchNormLayer` and as a base name for the `NonlinearityLayer`. If neither the wrapped layer is named, nor a name is specified in `kwargs`, the created layers could remain unnamed.

I could make a PR for this.

issue_title: Summary of db and mds changes considered
issue_body:
*An issue for tracking the API-breaking changes being considered in databroker and related libs*

To improve performance:
* [x] Break `db.__call__` API to return a generator or some generator-like lazy iterable

To make documents from RE and db match exactly:
* [x] Do not dereference by default. That is, currently `header.descriptors[0].start == header.start` but we would change to `header.descriptors[0].start == header.start.uid`. Likewise `event.descriptor` would become a uid.
* [ ] Do not fill in place by default (so that callbacks don't interfere with one another)

To avoid configuration-file spaghetti:

issue_title: Efficiently pulling latest message from a topic
issue_body:
I am interested in a method to efficiently pull the last message from an existing consumer

After a little bit of digging I've come up with the following incantation

```python
consumer = client = KafkaClient(hosts="xxxxxxx")
topic = client.topics['mytopic']
consumer = topic.get_simple_consumer()

def consume_latest():
     latest_offsets = [
           (partition, pykafka.common.OffsetType.LATEST) for partition in consumer._partitions.keys()
        ]
     consumer.reset_offsets(latest_offsets)
     # now step the offset back by one to fetch the latest...
     almost_latest_offsets = []
     for partition, owned_partition in consumer._partition.items():
         almost_latest_offsets.append(
              (partition, owned_partition.next_offset - 1)
          )

     consumer.reset_offsets(almost_latest_offsets)
     return consumer.consume()
```

which looks a little funky but to me but I _think_ it should work. However because of the funkiness I wanted to check if there might be better way to do it?

issue_title: "deblend_sources" properly deblends only the first blend it encounters
issue_body:
The deblending code in `deblend_sources` now properly deblends only the first blend it encounters, as it re-writes the reference to original `data` and therefore passes wrong image data to underlying `_deblend_source` function after first successful deblending. The following simple patch fixes it:

```diff
         if source_deblended.nlabels > 1:
             # replace the original source with the deblended source
             source_mask = (source_deblended.data > 0)
-            data = segm_deblended.data
-            data[source_slice][source_mask] = (
+            temp_data = segm_deblended.data
+            temp_data[source_slice][source_mask] = (
                 source_deblended.data[source_mask] + last_label)
-            segm_deblended.data = data    # needed to call data setter
+            segm_deblended.data = temp_data    # needed to call data setter
             last_label += source_deblended.nlabels
 
     if relabel:
```

issue_title: DynamoDBStreamReadPolicy is cumbersome to work with
issue_body:
Hi!

I'm spinning up a toy Go lambda that reads stream events from a DynamoDB table. Both are specified in the same CloudFormation template.

The [DynamoDBStreaReadPolicy](https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/blob/master/examples/2016-10-31/policy_templates/all_policy_templates.yaml#L60) requires the table name and a stream name. It looks like [it's constructing the `StreamArn` under the hood](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverlessrepo/latest/devguide/using-aws-sam.html#dynamo-db-stream-read-policy).

The DynamoDB table resource already includes a [return value for the `StreamArn`](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-dynamodb-table.html#w2ab2c21c10d336c15) but it doesn't look like it gives access to the stream's name. In fact given that it's some sort of timestamp it looks like it will be difficult to infer without creating the stream first.

Can the `DynamoDBStreaReadPolicy` be updated to accept the `StreamArn` directly?

issue_title: Move third_party.rst to new repo
issue_body:
I didn't know about this file in nbgrader's documentation:

```
nbgrader/nbgrader/docs/source/user_guide/third_party.rst
```

The page of links to resources is a great idea, but it is in the wrong place. These so-called "third party" links should be kept outside of nbgrader as many don't have much (or anything) to do with nbgrader.

Suggestions:

1. Create a new repo under the jupyter project called jupyter-in-education.
2. This could be a documentation-only repo
3. The existing page could be broken up into many subsections (extensions, cloud-based services, etc)
4. The term "third party" makes a distinction that is not that useful here (most jupyter-in-education resources don't come from the jupyter project itself)
5. link to this new repo from nbgrader

issue_title: sqlite database does not refresh
issue_body: If you have the formgrader running and then e.g. re-run nbgrader autograde, the formgrader does not always reflect the updates to the database. I think this is due to a cached connection to the database in the flask app, so this caching probably should be disabled.

issue_title: Add indicator that a cell has been modified since it has been last executed
issue_body: It is sometimes not clear whether a cell has been changed and therefore whether the code and the output are in sync. An easy way to do this might be to highlight the cell a certain color when it is edited and remove the highlight when it is executed.

issue_title: Notebook validation fails when using IPython.display.Audio
[MathJax] unsupported user configurations
issue_body:
We had a bug filed upstream at https://github.com/mathjax/MathJax/issues/1347 which seems to come down to Jupyter Notebooks not shipping all MathJax components, in particular not all output options.

If our educated guess is correct, we'd suggest to disable the corresponding MathJax UI elements to avoid users from accidentally breaking things for themselves.
